title: celtis philippensis var. wightii
intro: Celtis philippensis var. Wightii, also known as the Philippine hackberry, is a plant species belonging to the family Cannabaceae, native to the Philippines. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiabetic activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including lowering blood sugar levels, reducing cholesterol, and aiding in the management of conditions such as hypertension and diabetes. The herbal preparations of Celtis philippensis var. Wightii typically involve the leaves and bark being made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are then used to treat various ailments.
